safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live 2026-05-11 (textbook 9): scalp entry on a retest of the value-area-low (4,650-4,680). Levels are dollar gold-per-ounce as Zero charted them, NOT SGLN's GBP share price.

targets.basis: zero_live 2026-05-11 'full rotation' + zero_live 2026-04-20 POC / value-area-high confluence (5,000)

safest_plan.stop_basis: below the value-area-low boundary (~4,600-4,620, zero_live 2026-04-27 'close below value at low'); deeper structural break is the largest volume-profile low in the ~4,500s

stance_by_source.summary: Textbook continuation: already long from the value-area-low flip; buy retests of 4,650-4,680, first target the value-area-high 4,823, then a full rotation to 5,000; invalid on a close back below the value area.
Be patient for a pullback to the value-area-low at 4,600-4,620 to buy, aiming at the value-area-high; setup fails on a break and hold below the heavy-volume low in the 4,500s.
Buy the 4,600-4,700 rotation zone, targeting 5,000 (point of control / value-area-high) then 5,200 (top of the value area); invalid on a close below the value-area-low.
Older, lower read: accumulate gold in the 3,600-3,900 zone on a correction, targets 4,900-5,000; price has since rallied well above this, so the entry is stale.
Earliest read: average in around 3,600-3,900, targeting 4,200-4,500, with a weekly close below 3,500 as the line in the sand. Fully played out and well below current price.

conflicts: Only one source (Zero's live sessions) drives the entire read — there is no second, independent opinion to confirm it, so conviction is capped below the top band.

The two oldest reads (March 23 and April 8) quote gold buy zones of 3,600-3,900 and targets of 4,200-4,500 / 4,900-5,000; gold has since rallied to ~4,700-4,800, so those entries are stale and are superseded by the newer 4,600-4,700 value-area zone. Same direction, just outdated levels.
The charts behind the two oldest dates are actually SILVER charts, while their text describes gold — the gold levels there were spoken commentary, not drawn levels, so treat them as soft.

All levels here are dollar gold-per-ounce (what Zero charted on the Gold CFD), not SGLN's own GBP/pence share price, and live price is null, so the numbers cannot be mapped directly onto an SGLN order ticket without converting.
plain_english: Every read from this source points the same way: gold is in an uptrend and the play is to buy dips back into a support band rather than chase. The most recent and best-rated call (top marks) says: if price pulls back to roughly the 4,650-4,680 zone, buy it, aim first at about 4,823 and then a fuller move toward 5,000, and abandon the trade if it closes back below about 4,620. Two things keep this from being a high-confidence green light: it all comes from one source with no second opinion, and the price numbers are dollar gold-per-ounce, not SGLN's own pound-based share price, which we don't have here. With price already above the buy zone, the honest action is to wait for the pullback into 4,650-4,680 before entering — don't chase it higher.
